approach.title: Science becomes useful when it becomes local.
approach.lede: MARVI brings technical knowledge, community experience and practical monitoring together at village level.

bjs.title: The village’s own groundwater champions.
bjs.lede: “Groundwater informed”: local volunteers who connect careful observation with community knowledge and collective action.

groundwater.title: An invisible resource with visible consequences.
groundwater.lede: Rain moves through soil and rock into an aquifer. Wells reveal only a small window into a connected underground system.

mywell.title: Groundwater, brought to the surface.
mywell.lede: A public-domain, non-profit tool for collecting, viewing and sharing community water observations.

media.title: Stories from the field, the lab and the village.
media.lede: Selected journalism and project coverage from the new MARVI media archive.

films.title: See the people behind the measurements.
films.lede: A curated starting point from the new MARVI video archive—introductions, field stories, training and technical talks.

game.title: Share a well. Survive the year.
game.lede: A whole village draws from one aquifer. Every crop and every turn reveals why groundwater decisions cannot be made alone.

people.title: A transdisciplinary project, built across borders.
people.lede: Researchers, practitioners, institutions and village volunteers in India and Australia working as one team.

archive.title: Fieldwork, faces and the life of the project.
archive.lede: Every publicly accessible image recovered from the original MARVI website, preserved locally and arranged as a living visual archive.
